2605.19804.title: Stitched Value Model for Diffusion Alignment
2605.19804.paper_summary: For practical use, diffusion- or flow-based generative models must be aligned with task-specific rewards, such as prompt fidelity or aesthetic preference. That alignment is challenging because the reward is defined for clean output images, but the alignment procedure requires value function estimates at noisy intermediate latents. Existing methods resort to Tweedie-style or Monte Carlo approximations, trading off estimator bias against computational cost: Tweedie estimates are efficient but biased, while Monte Carlo estimates are more accurate but require expensive rollouts. A natural alternative would be a learned value function, but it remains an open question how to effectively train a strong and general value model specifically for noisy latents. Here, we propose StitchVM, a model stitching framework that efficiently transfers reward models pretrained for clean images to the noisy latent regime. StitchVM starts from an existing, truncated pixel-space reward model and attaches a frozen diffusion backbone to it as its head. From the pixel-space model, the resulting hybrid retains a carefully pretrained, robust reward capability; from the diffusion backbone, it inherits its native ability to handle noisy latents. The stitching procedure is exceptionally lightweight, e.g., stitching and finetuning CLIP ViT-L and SD 3.5 Medium takes only 10 GPU-hours. By lifting powerful pixel-space reward models to latent space, StitchVM opens up a new style of diffusion alignment: instead of rough, yet costly per-sample approximation of the value function, the correct function for the actual, noisy latents is constructed once and then amortized over many samples and iterations. We show that this approach yields improvements across a broad range of downstream steering and post-training methods: DPS becomes $3.2\times$ faster while halving peak GPU memory, and DiffusionNFT becomes $2.3\times$ faster.
2605.19804.paper_summary_zh: 對於實際應用，基於擴散或流的生成模型必須與特定任務的獎勵對齊，例如提示的真實性或美學偏好。這種對齊是具有挑戰性的，因為獎勵是針對乾淨的輸出圖像定義的，但對齊過程需要在嘈雜的中間潛變量上進行價值函數估計。現有的方法依賴於Tweedie風格或蒙地卡羅近似，權衡估計器偏差與計算成本：Tweedie估計是高效的但有偏差，而蒙地卡羅估計則更準確但需要昂貴的展開。自然的替代方案是學習的價值函數，但如何有效訓練一個強大且通用的價值模型專門針對嘈雜潛變量仍然是一個未解決的問題。在這裡，我們提出了StitchVM，一個模型拼接框架，能有效地將為乾淨圖像預訓練的獎勵模型轉移到嘈雜的潛變量範疇。StitchVM從現有的截斷像素空間獎勵模型開始，並將一個凍結的擴散骨幹作為其頭部附加上去。從像素空間模型中，結果的混合體保留了一個經過精心預訓練的穩健獎勵能力；從擴散骨幹中，它繼承了處理嘈雜潛變量的固有能力。拼接過程極其輕量，例如，拼接和微調CLIP ViT-L和SD 3.5 Medium僅需10個GPU小時。通過將強大的像素空間獎勵模型提升到潛變量空間，StitchVM開啟了一種新的擴散對齊風格：不再是粗略但昂貴的每樣本價值函數近似，而是為實際的嘈雜潛變量構建正確的函數，然後在多個樣本和迭代中攤銷。我們展示了這種方法在廣泛的下游引導和後訓練方法中帶來了改進：DPS變得快了$3.2\times$，同時將峰值GPU內存減半，而DiffusionNFT變得快了$2.3\times$。
2605.19804.author: Hyojun Go et.al.
2605.19804.authors: Hyojun Go, Hyungjin Chung, Prune Truong, Goutam Bhat, Li Mi, Zhaochong An, Zixiang Zhao, Dominik Narnhofer, Serge Belongie, Federico Tombari, Konrad Schindler
